def: a period during which something happens or continues

ex.en: What time is it?
I don't have much time.

def: a period of 24 hours

ex.en: Have a nice day!
We spent the whole day at the beach.
ex.ru: Мы провели весь день на пляже.

def: a period of 365 days

ex.en: This year has been very busy.
She visits her parents once a year.
ex.ru: Она навещает родителей раз в год.

def: men, women, and children in general

ex.en: Many people were waiting outside.
People in this city are very friendly.
ex.ru: Люди в этом городе очень дружелюбны.

def: a building where people live

ex.en: They bought a new house.
Our house is near the park.

def: a clear liquid needed for life

ex.en: Can I have a glass of water?
Drink more water every day.

def: things that people and animals eat

ex.en: The food here is delicious.
We need to buy some food.

def: a group of parents and children

ex.en: I love spending time with my family.
ex.ru: Я люблю проводить время с семьёй.

ex.en: Her family lives in another city.
ex.ru: Её семья живёт в другом городе.

def: a person you like and trust

ex.en: He is my best friend.
I made new friends at school.
ex.ru: Я завёл новых друзей в школе.

def: what a person or thing is called

ex.en: What's your name?
I forgot his name.

def: a large town

ex.en: London is a big city.
I live in a small city.

def: a nation with its own government

ex.en: Which country are you from?
This country has beautiful mountains.

def: a set of printed pages bound together to read

ex.en: I'm reading an interesting book.
She bought two books yesterday.

def: coins and banknotes used to pay for things

ex.en: I need to save more money.
He doesn't have much money.

def: a device used to talk to people over distance

ex.en: My phone battery is low.
Can you call me on my phone?

def: a place where children go to learn

ex.en: My son goes to school by bus.
ex.ru: Мой сын ездит в школу на автобусе.

ex.en: We studied together at school.

def: a person who teaches students

ex.en: Our teacher explains everything clearly.
She wants to become a teacher.

def: a person who is studying

ex.en: He is a good student.
The students are doing an exam today.

def: a person trained to treat sick people

ex.en: I need to see a doctor.
The doctor gave me some medicine.

def: a vehicle with four wheels used for travel

ex.en: He drives a red car.
We went there by car.

def: the early part of the day

ex.en: I drink coffee every morning.
Good morning, everyone!

def: the part of the day before night

ex.en: We usually eat dinner in the evening.
See you this evening!

def: the time when it is dark outside

ex.en: I couldn't sleep last night.
ex.ru: Я не мог уснуть прошлой ночью.

ex.en: It gets very quiet at night.

def: a period of seven days

ex.en: I'll see you next week.
She works five days a week.
ex.ru: Она работает пять дней в неделю.

def: one of the twelve parts of a year

ex.en: This is my first month here.
We travel every month.

def: the appearance things have, like red or blue

ex.en: What color do you like?
The dress comes in three colors.

def: the condition of the sky and air, such as rain or sun

ex.en: The weather is nice today.
I don't like cold weather.

def: a road in a town with houses along it

ex.en: The shop is on this street.
Be careful crossing the street.

def: a place where you buy things

ex.en: I bought bread at the shop.
The shop opens at nine.

def: work that you do to earn money

ex.en: She found a new job.
He does a great job.

def: large in size

ex.en: They live in a big house.
That's a big problem.

def: little in size

ex.en: I have a small apartment.
The dog is very small.

def: of high quality or pleasant

ex.en: That's a good idea.
He is good at football.

def: not good, of poor quality

ex.en: The weather is bad today.
I had a bad day.

def: feeling pleased or glad

ex.en: I'm so happy to see you.
She looks happy today.

def: having a high temperature

ex.en: This soup is very hot.
It's hot outside today.

def: having a low temperature

ex.en: The water is too cold.
Winters here are very cold.

def: recently made or bought

ex.en: I bought a new phone.
She started a new job.

def: having lived or existed for a long time

ex.en: This is an old building.
My grandfather is very old.

def: very pleasant to look at

ex.en: What a beautiful view!
She has a beautiful voice.

def: to put food in your mouth and swallow it

ex.en: Let's eat lunch together.
I don't eat meat.

def: to take liquid into your mouth and swallow it

ex.en: I drink tea every morning.
Do you want something to drink?

def: to rest with your eyes closed and mind inactive

ex.en: I need to sleep more.
The baby is sleeping now.

def: to move on foot at a normal speed

ex.en: We walked to the station.
I like to walk in the park.

def: to say words with your voice

ex.en: Can you speak English?
She speaks three languages.

def: to look at written words and understand them

ex.en: I read a book every week.
He likes to read before bed.

def: to put words on paper using a pen or keyboard

ex.en: I need to write an email.
She writes in her diary every day.
ex.ru: Она пишет в дневнике каждый день.

def: to get something by paying money for it

ex.en: I want to buy a new bag.
We bought some fruit at the market.

def: to do a job or task

ex.en: I work in an office.
She works very hard.

def: to have your home somewhere

ex.en: They live in a small town.
I have lived here for five years.

def: a trip from one place to another

ex.en: The journey took six hours.
We had a long journey home.
ex.ru: У нас был долгий путь домой.

def: knowledge or skill gained from doing something

ex.en: She has a lot of experience in teaching.
ex.ru: У неё большой опыт в преподавании.

ex.en: It was an unforgettable experience.

def: a chance to do something

ex.en: This job is a great opportunity.
I didn't have the opportunity to visit them.
ex.ru: У меня не было возможности навестить их.

def: a choice you make after thinking

ex.en: It was a difficult decision to make.
She made the right decision.

def: the natural world around us

ex.en: We should protect the environment.
Plastic waste harms the environment.

def: a person who lives next to you

ex.en: My neighbor is very friendly.
We invited our neighbors for dinner.

def: the way two people are connected

ex.en: They have a close relationship.
Good communication is key in a relationship.

def: something you do often, almost without thinking

ex.en: Smoking is a bad habit.
I have a habit of waking up early.

def: an activity you do for pleasure in free time

ex.en: Her hobby is painting.
Do you have any hobbies?

def: a period of rest from work or school

ex.en: We're going on holiday next week.
ex.ru: Мы едем в отпуск на следующей неделе.

ex.en: Christmas is my favorite holiday.

def: vehicles moving on a road

ex.en: The traffic was terrible this morning.
We got stuck in traffic.

def: a place where planes take off and land

ex.en: I'll pick you up at the airport.
The airport was very crowded.

def: bags and cases for traveling

ex.en: Please don't leave your luggage unattended.
ex.ru: Пожалуйста, не оставляйте багаж без присмотра.

ex.en: I lost my luggage at the airport.

def: an official document for traveling abroad

ex.en: Don't forget your passport.
I need to renew my passport.

def: a plan of times for events

ex.en: What's your schedule like today?
The meeting is on schedule.

def: an arranged time to meet someone

ex.en: I have a doctor's appointment tomorrow.
ex.ru: У меня завтра запись к врачу.

ex.en: Can I make an appointment for Friday?

def: a formal meeting to ask someone questions

ex.en: I have a job interview tomorrow.
The interview went really well.

def: money paid regularly for work

ex.en: She got a salary increase.
The salary here is quite good.

def: a person who buys goods or services

ex.en: The customer asked for a refund.
We always try to satisfy our customers.
ex.ru: Мы всегда стараемся угодить нашим клиентам.

def: a person who runs a business or team

ex.en: I need to speak to the manager.
She was promoted to manager.

def: a gathering of people to discuss something

ex.en: We have a meeting at 3 pm.
ex.ru: У нас встреча в 3 часа дня.

ex.en: The meeting lasted two hours.

def: a written or printed paper with information

ex.en: Please sign this document.
I saved the document on my computer.

def: a paper proving you paid for something

ex.en: Keep your receipt in case you need a refund.
Can I have a receipt, please?

def: instructions for cooking a dish

ex.en: This is my grandmother's recipe.
I found a new recipe for pasta.

def: one of the parts used to make food

ex.en: Sugar is the main ingredient in this cake.
We're missing one ingredient.
ex.ru: У нас не хватает одного ингредиента.

def: physical activity to stay healthy

ex.en: Regular exercise is good for your health.
I do exercise every morning.

def: the food a person usually eats

ex.en: She's on a healthy diet.
A balanced diet keeps you fit.

def: a disease or period of being sick

ex.en: He recovered quickly from his illness.
Stress can cause illness.

def: a substance used to treat illness

ex.en: Take this medicine twice a day.
ex.ru: Принимайте это лекарство дважды в день.

ex.en: The medicine helped me feel better.
ex.ru: Лекарство помогло мне почувствовать себя лучше.

def: a shop that sells medicine

ex.en: The pharmacy is next to the bank.
I bought some vitamins at the pharmacy.

def: giving a pleasant feeling of ease

ex.en: This chair is very comfortable.
I feel comfortable in this house.
ex.ru: Я чувствую себя комфортно в этом доме.

def: likely to cause harm

ex.en: It's dangerous to drive too fast.
That area is quite dangerous at night.

def: extremely tired

ex.en: I'm exhausted after work.
She felt exhausted after the trip.
ex.ru: Она чувствовала себя измождённой после поездки.

def: worried or afraid about something

ex.en: I always get nervous before exams.
He seemed nervous during the interview.

def: sure of your own abilities

ex.en: She is confident about her abilities.
He spoke in a confident voice.

def: full of people

ex.en: The train was very crowded.
This beach gets crowded in summer.

def: easy or suitable for your needs

ex.en: Is it convenient to meet tomorrow?
The location is very convenient.

def: made dirty by harmful substances

ex.en: The river is heavily polluted.
Air in big cities is often polluted.
ex.ru: Воздух в больших городах часто загрязнён.

def: able to be trusted

ex.en: He is a reliable friend.
This car is old but reliable.

def: willing to give money, help, or time

ex.en: It was very generous of you to help.
ex.ru: С твоей стороны было очень щедро помочь.

ex.en: She is generous with her time.

def: to reach a place

ex.en: We arrived at the hotel late at night.
ex.ru: Мы прибыли в отель поздно ночью.

ex.en: The train arrives at 5 pm.

def: to take something to return later

ex.en: Can I borrow your pen?
I borrowed some money from my brother.
ex.ru: Я занял немного денег у брата.

def: to give something to someone temporarily

ex.en: Could you lend me some money?
ex.ru: Не мог бы ты одолжить мне денег?

ex.en: He lent me his car for the weekend.
ex.ru: Он одолжил мне свою машину на выходные.

def: to say you are unhappy about something

ex.en: She complained about the noise.
He never complains about anything.
ex.ru: Он никогда ни на что не жалуется.

def: to offer an idea or plan

ex.en: I suggest we leave early.
She suggested a new plan.

def: to make or become better

ex.en: I want to improve my English.
Her health has improved a lot.

def: to form a picture in your mind

ex.en: Imagine living on the moon.
I can't imagine life without you.
ex.ru: Я не могу представить жизнь без тебя.

def: to say what something is like

ex.en: Can you describe the man you saw?
She described her trip in detail.

def: to look at differences and similarities

ex.en: Let's compare the two options.
You can't compare these two cities.

def: to arrange or plan something

ex.en: She organized the whole event.
We need to organize our schedule.

def: something successfully done through effort

ex.en: Winning the award was a great achievement.
ex.ru: Победа в награде стала большим достижением.

ex.en: She's proud of her achievements.

def: a strong desire to succeed

ex.en: His ambition is to become a doctor.
She has always had great ambition.
ex.ru: У неё всегда были большие амбиции.

def: the way you think or feel about something

ex.en: He has a positive attitude towards life.
ex.ru: У него позитивное отношение к жизни.

ex.en: Her attitude changed after the meeting.

def: the way a person acts

ex.en: The teacher praised his good behavior.
Her behavior surprised everyone.

def: a difficult task or situation

ex.en: Learning a new language is a big challenge.
She faced many challenges at work.
ex.ru: Она столкнулась со многими трудностями на работе.

def: a strong dedication to something

ex.en: Marriage requires real commitment.
He showed great commitment to the project.

def: a result of an action

ex.en: You must accept the consequences of your actions.
ex.ru: Ты должен принять последствия своих действий.

ex.en: The decision had serious consequences.

def: something you give or add to help

ex.en: Her contribution to the project was huge.
ex.ru: Её вклад в проект был огромным.

ex.en: Thank you for your contribution.

def: a strong desire to know or learn

ex.en: Curiosity is important for learning.
Out of curiosity, I opened the box.

def: firmness in achieving a goal

ex.en: She showed great determination to succeed.
ex.ru: Она проявила большую решимость добиться успеха.

ex.en: His determination helped him win.

def: controlled behavior following rules

ex.en: Playing an instrument requires discipline.
The team lacks discipline.

def: the ability to change or adapt easily

ex.en: This job offers a lot of flexibility.
Flexibility is important in yoga.

def: freedom from control by others

ex.en: She values her independence.
The country gained independence in 1960.

def: the power to affect someone or something

ex.en: Parents have a big influence on children.
His work had a lasting influence.

def: the reason or desire to do something

ex.en: I lack motivation to study today.
ex.ru: Мне не хватает мотивации учиться сегодня.

ex.en: Her motivation comes from her family.

def: a particular way of viewing things

ex.en: Try to see it from her perspective.
ex.ru: Постарайся взглянуть на это с её точки зрения.

ex.en: Travel gives you a new perspective on life.
ex.ru: Путешествия дают новый взгляд на жизнь.

def: the opinion people have about someone

ex.en: The company has a good reputation.
He ruined his reputation.

def: a duty to deal with something

ex.en: Taking care of pets is a big responsibility.
It's your responsibility to finish the task.

def: the ability to accept different views

ex.en: We should teach children tolerance.
He has zero tolerance for lies.

def: the quality of having good judgment

ex.en: Age brings wisdom.
She shared her wisdom with us.
ex.ru: Она поделилась с нами своей мудростью.

def: feeling worried or nervous

ex.en: I feel anxious about the results.
She was anxious before the flight.

def: having a strong desire to succeed

ex.en: He is an ambitious young man.
Their plan is very ambitious.

def: having an exaggerated sense of importance

ex.en: He can be quite arrogant sometimes.
Her arrogant attitude annoyed everyone.

def: careful to avoid danger

ex.en: Be cautious when crossing the road.
He gave a cautious answer.

def: eager to know or learn something

ex.en: Children are naturally curious.
I'm curious about your trip.
ex.ru: Мне любопытно узнать о твоей поездке.

def: having firmly decided to do something

ex.en: She is determined to win the race.
He remained determined despite the setbacks.
ex.ru: Он оставался решительным несмотря на неудачи.

def: able to change easily

ex.en: My work schedule is flexible.
You need to be flexible when traveling.
ex.ru: Нужно быть гибким во время путешествий.

def: real and sincere

ex.en: She gave a genuine smile.
This is a genuine leather bag.

def: not proud or arrogant

ex.en: Despite his success, he stayed humble.
ex.ru: Несмотря на успех, он остался скромным.

ex.en: She comes from a humble background.

def: refusing to change your mind

ex.en: My grandfather is very stubborn.
Don't be so stubborn, listen to advice.
ex.ru: Не будь таким упрямым, прислушайся к совету.

def: to succeed in doing something

ex.en: She accomplished her goal.
We accomplished a lot this week.
ex.ru: Мы многое сделали на этой неделе.

def: to accept or admit that something is true

ex.en: He acknowledged his mistake.
She acknowledged the letter.

def: to expect something before it happens

ex.en: We anticipate some delays.
I didn't anticipate this problem.

def: to believe something without proof

ex.en: I assumed you already knew.
ex.ru: Я предположил, что ты уже знал.

ex.en: Let's not assume anything.

def: to make someone believe something

ex.en: She convinced me to join.
He couldn't convince his boss.
ex.ru: Он не смог убедить своего начальника.

def: to work together with others

ex.en: The two teams agreed to cooperate.
Please cooperate with the police.

def: to express disapproval of someone or something

ex.en: He always criticizes my work.
Don't criticize her in public.

def: to give special importance to something

ex.en: She emphasized the importance of sleep.
I want to emphasize one point.

def: to pause before doing or saying something

ex.en: Don't hesitate to call me.
She hesitated before answering.

def: to discuss to reach an agreement

ex.en: They negotiated a better price.
We need to negotiate the terms.

def: to succeed in dealing with a problem

ex.en: She overcame her fear of flying.
We will overcome this challenge.

def: to make someone agree to do something

ex.en: He persuaded me to change my mind.
She tried to persuade her parents.

def: to think carefully about something

ex.en: Take time to reflect on your choices.
ex.ru: Найди время поразмышлять о своём выборе.

ex.en: The lake reflected the mountains.

def: to find a solution to a problem

ex.en: We need to resolve this issue quickly.
ex.ru: Нам нужно быстро решить эту проблему.

ex.en: They resolved their differences.

def: to give up something valuable for something else

ex.en: She sacrificed her free time to help.
ex.ru: Она пожертвовала свободным временем, чтобы помочь.

ex.en: Parents often sacrifice a lot for their kids.
ex.ru: Родители часто многим жертвуют ради детей.

def: to accept something you dislike without complaint

ex.en: I won't tolerate this behavior.
She can't tolerate loud noise.

def: to experience something, often difficult

ex.en: He underwent surgery last week.
ex.ru: На прошлой неделе он перенёс операцию.

ex.en: The city has undergone many changes.

def: to survive or resist something difficult

ex.en: This material can withstand high heat.
She withstood a lot of pressure.

def: to give good reasons for something

ex.en: He tried to justify his decision.
The cost is hard to justify.

def: to treat something as more important

ex.en: You need to prioritize your tasks.
ex.ru: Тебе нужно расставить приоритеты в задачах.

ex.en: She prioritizes her health over work.

def: the quality of having more than one meaning

ex.en: There's some ambiguity in his answer.
ex.ru: В его ответе есть некоторая неоднозначность.

ex.en: The contract was full of ambiguity.

def: something believed to be true without proof

ex.en: That's just an assumption, not a fact.
ex.ru: Это всего лишь предположение, а не факт.

ex.en: We based our plan on a wrong assumption.
ex.ru: Мы основали план на неверном предположении.

def: an unfair preference for or against something

ex.en: The report showed clear bias.
Try to judge without bias.

def: the quality of being logical and consistent

ex.en: The essay lacks coherence.
She improved the coherence of her argument.

def: public disagreement about a topic

ex.en: The decision caused a lot of controversy.
There is ongoing controversy about the policy.

def: the quality of being trusted or believed

ex.en: The scandal damaged his credibility.
She has a lot of credibility in the field.
ex.ru: Она пользуется большим авторитетом в этой сфере.

def: a difference between things that should match

ex.en: There's a discrepancy between the two reports.
We found a discrepancy in the accounts.

def: a situation requiring a choice between hard options

ex.en: She faced a difficult dilemma.
This is a moral dilemma.

def: a possible effect or result

ex.en: We must consider the implications of this policy.
ex.ru: Мы должны учесть последствия этой политики.

ex.en: His words carried a hidden implication.
ex.ru: В его словах был скрытый подтекст.

def: something that encourages an action

ex.en: The company offers incentives for good work.
ex.ru: Компания предлагает стимулы за хорошую работу.

ex.en: There's little incentive to change.

def: the quality of being honest and having strong morals

ex.en: He is a man of great integrity.
She never compromised her integrity.
ex.ru: Она никогда не поступалась своей честностью.

def: the quality of being lawful or justified

ex.en: They questioned the legitimacy of the election.
ex.ru: Они поставили под сомнение легитимность выборов.

ex.en: The government's legitimacy was challenged.

def: a statement that seems contradictory but may be true

ex.en: It's a paradox that he's lonely in a crowded city.
ex.ru: Парадокс, что он одинок в переполненном городе.

ex.en: This situation is a classic paradox.

def: the way someone understands or sees something

ex.en: Public perception of the issue has changed.
Her perception of the event was different.

def: a fact or event, especially an unusual one

ex.en: This is a common phenomenon in nature.
Social media is a modern phenomenon.

def: the quality of seeming reasonable or likely true

ex.en: The plausibility of his story is questionable.
Scientists tested the plausibility of the theory.

def: an earlier event used as an example for later cases

ex.en: This case sets a legal precedent.
There is no precedent for this situation.

def: how common something is

ex.en: The prevalence of the disease has increased.
Researchers studied the prevalence of the habit.

def: the reasons behind a decision

ex.en: What's the rationale behind this decision?
He explained the rationale for the change.

def: close and careful examination

ex.en: The plan came under close scrutiny.
Her work was subject to scrutiny.
ex.ru: Её работа была под пристальным вниманием.

def: having mixed feelings about something

ex.en: I feel ambivalent about the decision.
ex.ru: Я испытываю двойственные чувства по поводу этого решения.

ex.en: She was ambivalent about moving abroad.
ex.ru: Она сомневалась насчёт переезда за границу.

def: based on random choice, not reason

ex.en: The rule seems completely arbitrary.
They made an arbitrary decision.

def: logical and clear

ex.en: He gave a coherent explanation.
Her argument was clear and coherent.
ex.ru: Её аргумент был ясным и последовательным.

def: causing public disagreement

ex.en: It's a controversial topic.
The film received controversial reviews.

def: able to be believed

ex.en: She is a credible witness.
We need credible evidence.

def: done on purpose

ex.en: It was a deliberate mistake.
His silence seemed deliberate.

def: certain to happen

ex.en: Change is inevitable.
The conflict was inevitable.

def: allowed by law or acceptable

ex.en: She has a legitimate reason to be upset.
ex.ru: У неё есть законная причина расстраиваться.

ex.en: Is this a legitimate business?

def: seeming reasonable or probable

ex.en: That explanation sounds plausible.
We need a more plausible theory.

def: no longer needed

ex.en: Some of these words are redundant.
His position became redundant.

def: not obvious, delicate

ex.en: There's a subtle difference between the two.
She gave a subtle hint.

def: capable of working successfully

ex.en: Is this plan financially viable?
They found a viable solution.

def: to express thoughts clearly

ex.en: She articulated her ideas clearly.
He struggled to articulate his feelings.
ex.ru: Ему было трудно выразить свои чувства.

def: to give something to make up for a loss

ex.en: The company compensated him for the damage.
Nothing can compensate for lost time.
ex.ru: Ничто не может компенсировать потерянное время.

def: to form or make up something

ex.en: Women constitute half of the workforce.
This action constitutes a crime.

def: to say the opposite of something

ex.en: His statement contradicts the facts.
Don't contradict yourself.

def: to recognize a difference between things

ex.en: It's hard to differentiate between the two brands.
Children learn to differentiate colors early.

def: to explain in more detail

ex.en: Could you elaborate on that point?
ex.ru: Не мог бы ты подробнее объяснить этот момент?

ex.en: She elaborated on her plan.
ex.ru: Она подробно рассказала о своём плане.

def: to make a problem worse

ex.en: The delay exacerbated the problem.
Stress can exacerbate health issues.
ex.ru: Стресс может усугубить проблемы со здоровьем.

def: to make a process easier

ex.en: Technology facilitates communication.
The teacher facilitated the discussion.

def: to include something as part of a whole

ex.en: The new plan incorporates several ideas.
We incorporated your feedback.

def: to explain the meaning of something

ex.en: How do you interpret this data?
She interpreted his silence as agreement.
ex.ru: Она истолковала его молчание как согласие.

def: to make something less severe

ex.en: We took steps to mitigate the risk.
ex.ru: Мы приняли меры, чтобы снизить риск.

ex.en: The plan aims to mitigate climate change.
ex.ru: План направлен на смягчение изменения климата.

def: to strengthen something

ex.en: The results reinforce our theory.
Practice reinforces learning.

def: to provide evidence to support a claim

ex.en: She couldn't substantiate her claims.
ex.ru: Она не смогла подтвердить свои утверждения.

ex.en: The evidence substantiates the theory.

def: to weaken something gradually

ex.en: His comments undermined her confidence.
The scandal undermined public trust.

def: to confirm that something is true or acceptable

ex.en: The test results validate our hypothesis.
Please validate your ticket before boarding.

def: to gather or collect over time

ex.en: Dust accumulated on the shelves.
She accumulated a lot of experience over the years.
ex.ru: За годы она накопила большой опыт.

def: to have a mutual relationship

ex.en: Income tends to correlate with education.
ex.ru: Доход, как правило, коррелирует с образованием.

ex.en: The two factors correlate closely.

def: to form a theory without firm evidence

ex.en: Analysts speculate about future prices.
ex.ru: Аналитики строят предположения о будущих ценах.

ex.en: I don't want to speculate without facts.
ex.ru: Я не хочу строить догадки без фактов.

def: the ability to make good judgments quickly

ex.en: She has great business acumen.
His acumen helped the company grow.

def: difficult or unfavorable circumstances

ex.en: He remained calm in the face of adversity.
ex.ru: Он оставался спокоен перед лицом трудностей.

ex.en: She overcame great adversity in her life.
ex.ru: Она преодолела большие трудности в своей жизни.

def: selfless concern for others' wellbeing

ex.en: Her charity work reflects true altruism.
ex.ru: Её благотворительная работа отражает подлинный альтруизм.

ex.en: Pure altruism is rare in business.

def: the right to govern or control yourself

ex.en: Teenagers seek more autonomy.
The region was granted autonomy.

def: awareness or knowledge of something

ex.en: We must take cognizance of the risks.
She acted with full cognizance of the facts.

def: a confusing and difficult problem

ex.en: This is a real conundrum for scientists.
We face a moral conundrum.

def: written or spoken communication on a topic

ex.en: Public discourse on the topic has grown.
ex.ru: Общественная дискуссия на эту тему усилилась.

ex.en: His discourse was full of complex ideas.
ex.ru: Его рассуждение было полно сложных идей.

def: a great difference between things

ex.en: There's a huge disparity in income.
The report highlighted disparities between regions.

def: a division into two opposite parts

ex.en: There's a dichotomy between theory and practice.
ex.ru: Существует противопоставление между теорией и практикой.

ex.en: The film explores the dichotomy of good and evil.
ex.ru: Фильм исследует дихотомию добра и зла.

def: a perfect example of something

ex.en: She is the epitome of elegance.
This restaurant is the epitome of luxury.

def: an urgent need or demand

ex.en: The exigencies of the situation required quick action.
They acted due to financial exigency.
ex.ru: Они действовали из-за крайней финансовой необходимости.

def: dominance of one group over others

ex.en: The empire maintained hegemony for centuries.
Cultural hegemony shapes public opinion.

def: a tendency to remain unchanged or inactive

ex.en: The company suffered from organizational inertia.
It's hard to overcome inertia and start exercising.
ex.ru: Трудно преодолеть инертность и начать заниматься спортом.

def: the placing of things side by side for comparison

ex.en: The juxtaposition of old and new buildings is striking.
ex.ru: Сопоставление старых и новых зданий впечатляет.

ex.en: The artist used juxtaposition of colors.

def: a subtle difference in meaning or expression

ex.en: Translation often loses subtle nuances.
She understood the nuances of the argument.

def: dealing with things in a practical way

ex.en: His pragmatism helped solve the problem quickly.
ex.ru: Его прагматизм помог быстро решить проблему.

ex.en: The team valued pragmatism over ideology.

def: rapid increase in number

ex.en: The proliferation of smartphones changed society.
There's growing concern about nuclear proliferation.
ex.ru: Растёт беспокойство по поводу распространения ядерного оружия.

def: an indirect effect of an event

ex.en: The decision had serious repercussions.
There will be repercussions for breaking the rule.

def: the ability to recover quickly from difficulty

ex.en: Her resilience helped her recover quickly.
ex.ru: Её стойкость помогла ей быстро восстановиться.

ex.en: The economy showed remarkable resilience.

def: the combination of ideas to form a whole

ex.en: The book is a synthesis of many theories.
Protein synthesis occurs in cells.

def: severely simple and plain

ex.en: He lived an austere life.
The room had an austere design.

def: changing mood or behavior suddenly

ex.en: The weather here is quite capricious.
Her capricious mood confused everyone.
ex.ru: Её капризное настроение всех сбивало с толку.

def: clear, logical, and convincing

ex.en: He made a cogent argument.
The report offers a cogent analysis.

def: showing good judgment or taste

ex.en: She has a discerning eye for art.
ex.ru: У неё наметанный глаз на искусство.

ex.en: Discerning customers demand quality.

def: open to more than one interpretation

ex.en: His answer was rather equivocal.
The results were equivocal.

def: understood by only a small group

ex.en: The lecture was too esoteric for beginners.
ex.ru: Лекция была слишком сложной для новичков.

ex.en: He collects esoteric books.

def: not favoring one side over another

ex.en: The judge must remain impartial.
We need an impartial opinion.

def: very careful and precise about details

ex.en: She is meticulous about her work.
He kept meticulous records.

def: present or spreading everywhere

ex.en: Social media has a pervasive influence.
The smell was pervasive throughout the house.

def: very weak or slight

ex.en: The evidence for this theory is tenuous.
Their connection to the case is tenuous.
ex.ru: Их связь с делом весьма сомнительна.

def: to become less intense

ex.en: The storm finally began to abate.
Her anger slowly abated.

def: to accept something reluctantly without protest

ex.en: He acquiesced to their demands.
She reluctantly acquiesced.

def: to make a situation better

ex.en: The new policy aims to ameliorate poverty.
ex.ru: Новая политика направлена на смягчение бедности.

ex.en: They tried to ameliorate the situation.

def: to find a way around an obstacle

ex.en: They found a way to circumvent the rule.
He circumvented the problem cleverly.

def: to combine two ideas into one, often mistakenly

ex.en: People often conflate these two ideas.
ex.ru: Люди часто смешивают эти два понятия.

ex.en: Don't conflate opinion with fact.

def: to confirm or support a statement

ex.en: The witness corroborated her story.
New evidence corroborates the theory.

def: to describe or outline precisely

ex.en: The contract delineates each party's duties.
ex.ru: Контракт чётко определяет обязанности каждой стороны.

ex.en: The report delineates the main risks.

def: to express disapproval of something

ex.en: The critic deprecated the new style.
This function has been deprecated.

def: to spread information widely

ex.en: The organization disseminates information widely.
News spreads fast when disseminated online.

def: to deliberately avoid something

ex.en: She eschews junk food.
He eschews public attention.

ru: экстраполировать, делать выводы на основе данных
def: to estimate by extending known data

ex.en: We can extrapolate future trends from this data.
ex.ru: Мы можем экстраполировать будущие тенденции из этих данных.

ex.en: It's risky to extrapolate from a small sample.
ex.ru: Рискованно делать выводы по небольшой выборке.

def: to shock someone into action

ex.en: The speech galvanized the crowd.
The crisis galvanized the community into action.

def: to cause something to start

ex.en: He was accused of instigating the riot.
ex.ru: Его обвинили в подстрекательстве к беспорядкам.

ex.en: She instigated changes in the company.

def: to spread through every part of something

ex.en: The smell of coffee permeated the room.
Fear permeated the entire city.

def: to increase rapidly in number

ex.en: Fake news has proliferated online.
ex.ru: Фейковые новости быстро распространились в интернете.

ex.en: Small businesses proliferated in the area.
ex.ru: Малый бизнес быстро развивался в этом районе.

def: to make two ideas or people agree again

ex.en: They finally reconciled after years apart.
ex.ru: Они наконец помирились после многих лет разлуки.

ex.en: It's hard to reconcile these two views.

def: to include something within a larger category

ex.en: The small company was subsumed into a larger one.
ex.ru: Маленькая компания была поглощена более крупной.

ex.en: This rule is subsumed under a broader principle.
ex.ru: Это правило входит в более широкий принцип.

def: to go beyond the limits of something

ex.en: Great art transcends cultural boundaries.
ex.ru: Великое искусство выходит за пределы культурных границ.

ex.en: Their friendship transcended language barriers.

def: to prove someone right or innocent

ex.en: The evidence vindicated her claims.
He was finally vindicated after the trial.

def: to emphasize the importance of something

ex.en: The report underscores the need for reform.
This event underscores a growing problem.

def: a solution that solves all problems

ex.en: There's no panacea for economic problems.
ex.ru: Не существует панацеи от экономических проблем.

ex.en: Technology is not a panacea for every issue.
ex.ru: Технологии — не панацея от всех проблем.

def: a very large amount of something

ex.en: There's a plethora of options available.
The store offers a plethora of choices.

def: a state of uncertainty about what to do

ex.en: She was in a quandary about what to do.
ex.ru: Она была в затруднении насчёт того, что делать.

ex.en: We found ourselves in a real quandary.
ex.ru: Мы оказались в настоящем затруднительном положении.

def: a change of circumstances, often difficult

ex.en: He endured the vicissitudes of life with grace.
ex.ru: Он с достоинством переносил превратности жизни.

ex.en: The vicissitudes of fortune affected them all.

def: the general mood or spirit of a time period

ex.en: The film captures the zeitgeist of the decade.
ex.ru: Фильм отражает дух времени того десятилетия.

ex.en: Her music reflects the current zeitgeist.
ex.ru: Её музыка отражает современный дух времени.

def: the use of tricks to deceive people

ex.en: The deal was full of legal chicanery.
Voters were tired of political chicanery.

def: the ability to understand things clearly

ex.en: Her perspicacity impressed the whole team.
He solved it with remarkable perspicacity.
ex.ru: Он решил это с поразительной проницательностью.

def: good judgment and wisdom

ex.en: The old man was known for his sagacity.
Her sagacity guided the company through crisis.
ex.ru: Её прозорливость провела компанию через кризис.

def: the act of making something unclear

ex.en: The report was full of obfuscation.
His answer seemed like deliberate obfuscation.

def: calmness under pressure

ex.en: She faced the crisis with equanimity.
He accepted the news with equanimity.

def: a casual lack of concern

ex.en: His insouciance annoyed his colleagues.
She spoke with youthful insouciance.

def: the tendency to lie

ex.en: The politician was known for his mendacity.
The book exposes the mendacity of the regime.

def: stubborn refusal to change

ex.en: His obduracy made negotiations difficult.
Her obduracy surprised everyone at the meeting.
ex.ru: Её упрямство удивило всех на встрече.

def: harsh public criticism or disgrace

ex.en: He faced public opprobrium after the scandal.
ex.ru: После скандала он столкнулся с общественным осуждением.

ex.en: The decision brought opprobrium on the company.

def: extreme poverty

ex.en: The family lived in penury for years.
He died in penury and obscurity.
ex.ru: Он умер в нищете и безвестности.

def: reckless waste of money or resources

ex.en: The government was criticized for its profligacy.
His profligacy left him deep in debt.
ex.ru: Его расточительность оставила его в глубоких долгах.

def: extreme self-centered focus

ex.en: The novel explores themes of solipsism.
His solipsism made him ignore others' feelings.
ex.ru: Его эгоцентризм заставлял его игнорировать чувства других.

def: a person who flatters others for personal gain

ex.en: He was surrounded by sycophants.
She despised sycophants at work.

def: the appearance of being true or real

ex.en: The film is praised for its verisimilitude.
The novel's verisimilitude impressed critics.

def: the power of choosing or willing

ex.en: She left of her own volition.
He acted against his own volition.

def: present or existing everywhere

ex.en: Smartphones are ubiquitous these days.
Coffee shops are ubiquitous in this city.
ex.ru: Кофейни есть на каждом шагу в этом городе.

def: very attentive to detail and cleanliness

ex.en: He is fastidious about cleanliness.
ex.ru: Он очень привередлив в вопросах чистоты.

ex.en: She's fastidious about her appearance.
ex.ru: Она очень требовательна к своему внешнему виду.

def: excessively eager to please or obey

ex.en: The waiter was almost obsequious.
His obsequious behavior annoyed the manager.

def: causing gradual but serious harm

ex.en: Social media can have a pernicious effect.
The disease had a pernicious impact on the economy.
ex.ru: Болезнь оказала губительное влияние на экономику.

def: stubbornly resistant to authority

ex.en: The recalcitrant child refused to listen.
He remained recalcitrant despite warnings.
ex.ru: Он оставался упрямым несмотря на предупреждения.

def: optimistic in a difficult situation

ex.en: She remained sanguine about the future.
He's sanguine despite the setbacks.
ex.ru: Он настроен оптимистично, несмотря на неудачи.

def: done secretly

ex.en: He took a surreptitious glance at his phone.
They had a surreptitious meeting.

def: saying very little

ex.en: He is a taciturn man who rarely smiles.
ex.ru: Он молчаливый человек, который редко улыбается.

ex.en: Her taciturn nature made her hard to know.
ex.ru: Её неразговорчивость затрудняла знакомство с ней.

def: full of bitter criticism

ex.en: The critic wrote a vitriolic review.
His vitriolic comments shocked the audience.

def: charming and attractive

ex.en: She has a winsome smile.
The child's winsome personality charmed everyone.

def: to formally cancel a law or agreement

ex.en: The government abrogated the old treaty.
They abrogated their responsibilities.

def: to describe roughly, without full detail

ex.en: The report adumbrates future challenges.
ex.ru: Отчёт обрисовывает будущие трудности в общих чертах.

ex.en: She adumbrated her plan briefly.

def: to complain about small, unimportant things

ex.en: He caviled at every small detail.
Critics caviled about minor errors.

def: to criticize unfairly, damaging reputation

ex.en: He denigrated his rival's achievements.
She refused to denigrate her former employer.
ex.ru: Она отказалась порочить своего бывшего работодателя.

def: to hide your true feelings or intentions

ex.en: He dissembled his true feelings.
She is not one to dissemble.
ex.ru: Она не из тех, кто притворяется.

def: to criticize someone severely

ex.en: The critics excoriated the new policy.
He was excoriated in the press.

def: to clear someone of blame

ex.en: New evidence exculpated the suspect.
He tried to exculpate himself.

def: to destroy something completely

ex.en: The campaign aims to extirpate corruption.
They worked to extirpate the disease.

def: to criticize something strongly in speech

ex.en: He inveighed against corruption.
She inveighed against the new law.

def: to make something confusing on purpose

ex.en: The lawyer tried to obfuscate the issue.
His speech only obfuscated the matter further.
ex.ru: Его речь ещё больше запутала вопрос.

def: to remove the need for something

ex.en: This step obviates the need for further tests.
ex.ru: Этот шаг устраняет необходимость в дальнейших тестах.

ex.en: Good planning obviates many problems.

def: to make symptoms less severe without curing them

ex.en: The medicine palliates the symptoms.
Nothing could palliate her grief.
ex.ru: Ничто не могло смягчить её горе.

def: to make someone less angry

ex.en: He tried to placate the angry customer.
She placated the crowd with a calm speech.

def: to forbid something officially

ex.en: The law proscribes such behavior.
Smoking is proscribed in this building.

def: to reject or deny something

ex.en: He repudiated the accusations.
She repudiated her earlier statement.
ex.ru: Она отреклась от своего прежнего заявления.

def: to bring under control by force

ex.en: The empire subjugated neighboring tribes.
He refused to be subjugated by fear.

def: to replace something, especially something older

ex.en: Digital media has supplanted print newspapers.
New technology supplanted the old system.

def: to shorten by cutting off part

ex.en: The article was truncated for space.
The program truncates long file names.

def: to speak of someone in a very negative way

ex.en: He was vilified in the media.
She refused to vilify her opponent.

def: to spoil or invalidate something

ex.en: The error vitiates the whole contract.
ex.ru: Эта ошибка делает весь контракт недействительным.

ex.en: Corruption vitiated the entire process.
